Income and Packaging

Guidance on the required documentation for your customer's mortgage application

Broker Portal log in

ONLY FOR USE BY MORTGAGE INTERMEDIARIES

On this page
A-Z income and packaging
Sending us packaging
Proof of deposit
Name and address identification
Next steps
Broker Portal log in

Sending us packaging

What to provide and by when

For New Business and Additional Borrowing applications, the Broker Portal will tell you exactly what documents you need to upload once you submit your application to us.

Once we have received all of the requested documents, your application will be sent to our underwriters and assessed within our processing timescales.

To help avoid delays in the underwriting assessment, please upload only the documents the Portal asks for, using the correct customer and relevant headers.

For New Business applications, you will have 14 calendar days to upload the full requested packaging. The day the case is submitted to us counts as day 1.

For Additional Borrowing applications, you will have 21 calendar days to upload the full requested packaging.

If we don’t receive the full packaging within the applicable timescale, you will need to rekey the application.

Tips for sending us packaging

We're able to accept scanned documents. Did you know there are a range of free scanning apps, such as Adobe Scan or CamScanner that can also help save you time and improve the accuracy and quality of your case packaging. Here's a few examples of how apps can help:

If you have lots of individual pages, it can help you collate them into one document.

If your client sends you payslips and bank statements as one document, it will allow you to scan them separately and upload the correct document to the correct requirement.

Most scanning apps are free and easy to use, helping reduce the time it takes for your customers to get their mortgage offer.

To download, please visit the appropriate App store for your device.

When we’ll need bank statements

For all applications, we may request Bank Statements alongside the packaging guidance below.  
If bank statements are requested, we’ll need 1-3 months bank statements for the customers main billing account, which will be where the customers’ regular payments are paid from.  

For new business and additional borrowing applications, the Broker Portal will confirm if and how many bank statements we need when you submit a full application.  

Reduced packaging requirements for Basic Earnings

For residential applications where you are using Basic Earnings only, we only need you to send 1 months payslip instead of 3 months’ worth. In some instances, we may even be able to electronically verify your customers basic income and won’t require any payslips. Please see Automated Income Verification in the A-Z below for more information. 

Basic Earnings types for packaging purposes are:

  • Armed Forces Interim Pay

  • Basic Income (this includes where customers employed by a family business & have less than 20% shares)

  • Car Allowance

  • Employer Housing Allowance

  • Employer Value Account

  • Long Term Large Town Allowance (Location Allowance)

  • Private pension (if it’s their only income)

If the customer has declared Basic Earnings along with a variable or other income type e.g. overtime, bonus, or other taxable income, we will require 3 months consecutive payslips.

 

For new business and additional borrowing applications, we will tell you exactly what documents to send when you submit an application to us.

Submitting a Buy To Let Application?

Please also see our Buy To Let Hubs for further packaging guidance:

  • Small and Portfolio landlords
  • First-time Buyers and Non-Owner Occupiers

Search our A - Z income and packaging

A-B, C, D-G, G-J, L-N, O-R, S-T, U-Z

Income and packaging A-B

Adoption allowance Close
Close

Adoption allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Other non-taxable income

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award letter or Latest 3 months consecutive Bank Statements showing credits

Close
Armed Forces Interim Pay Close
Close

Armed Forces Interim Pay

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence for residential New Business and Additional Borrowing applications: 0-3 months payslips. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many we need.

 

Evidence for Buy To Let applications: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

Close
Automated Income Verification Close
Close

Automated Income Verification

In some instances, we may be able to verify your customers income electronically.

 

When you submit an application, the Portal will tell you exactly what documents you need to upload. If a customer's income can be electronically verified, we won’t ask you for any payslips.

 

Note: Automated Income Verification is only used for new business residential applications.

Close
Basic Salary Close
Close

Basic Salary

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence for residential New Business and Additional Borrowing applications: 0-3 months payslips. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many we need.

 

Evidence for Buy To Let applications: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

 

Further information: Probationary periods can be considered.

Close
Bereavement Allowance Close
Close

Bereavement Allowance

Acceptable?  No

 

Previously Widows Pension.

Close
Bonus Close
Close

Bonus

In this section we cover monthly, quarterly and annual bonus packaging:

 

Monthly bonus

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Discretionary bonus and select correct frequency

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips showing bonus payments. If paid weekly, we need the latest 13 Payslips

Further information:

We can consider 100% of regular bonus evidenced by the last 3 months consecutive payslips. Monthly income should correlate to YTD salary.

 

Quarterly bonus

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Discretionary bonus and select correct frequency

Evidence: Latest 4 Payslips showing bonus payment for each quarter

Further information:

We can consider 100% of quarterly bonus evidenced by the latest 4 Payslips showing the bonus payment for each quarter.

 

Annual or Bi-annual discretionary bonus – Max 50%

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Discretionary bonus and select correct frequency

Evidence: Payslips showing bonus for last 2 years. 2 Payslips where annual or 4 Payslips where bi-annual

Further information:

• Income has increased, we will calculate the average income over the last 2 years.

• Income has decreased, we will use the most recent year's income, where it reflects a lower figure.

For affordability calculations, 50% of this income will be considered. When it comes to discretionary bonuses, we will average the cash elements of bonuses received over the last 2 years.

When keying our calculator and application, please input 100% of the bonus amount based on the above calculations.

 

Annual or Bi-annual guaranteed bonus – 100%

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Guaranteed bonus and select correct frequency

Evidence: Payslips showing bonus for last 2 years. 2 Payslips where annual or 4 Payslips where bi-annual. Contract also required

Further information:

• Income has increased, we will calculate the average income over the last 2 years.

• Income has decreased, we will use the most recent year's income, where it reflects a lower figure.

 

For affordability calculations, 100% of this income will be considered. When it comes to discretionary bonuses, we will average the cash elements of bonuses received over the last 2 years.

When keying our calculator and application, please input 100% of the bonus amount based on the above calculations. We will require the contract to confirm there is no stipulations on the bonus being paid.

Close
Bursary (From employer) Close
Close

Bursary (From employer)

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Other taxable income

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips & Employment reference

Further information: Must be paid for living expenses and not for fees

Close

Income and packaging C

Cabin/Pilot Crew Close
Close

Cabin/Pilot Crew

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment or Other income section

Select option: Key according to relevant income type received e.g. Shift allowance

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive pay slips. YTD income should support

Further information:

Pilot/Cabin crew can have multiple elements to their income that may also benefit from tax relief, which is acceptable. Please note that expenses payments or reimbursement for costs can't be used as income.

Close
Car Allowance Close
Close

Car Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Car allowance

Evidence for residential New Business and Additional Borrowing applications: 0-3 months payslips. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many we need.

 

Evidence for Buy To Let applications: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

Close
Carer’s Allowance Close
Close

Carer’s Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Carer's allowance

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award letter

Further information:

Carer's Allowance is paid to people aged 16 or over spending at least 35 hours per week caring for someone with substantial caring needs. We accept 100% of a Carer’s Allowance.

Close
Child Benefit Close
Close

Child Benefit

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Child benefit

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award Letter or Latest 1-3 months Consecutive Bank Statements showing credits. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many bank statements we need.

Further information:

We can accept up to 100% of Child Benefit.

We require the awards letter as part of the application packaging. Where the child is 16 or over on the award letter, you shouldn't include this income unless you can evidence that the income will continue. ​

Where the income is being excluded and the child is no longer financially dependent, you can also exclude them as a dependent from the application.

If any party to the application receives an income of £60,000 or more - Child Benefit must not be included.

Please note the customer(s) named on the award statement must match the application.

Click below for more information on:
• Working Tax Credits 
• Universal Credit

Close
Child Tax Credit Close
Close

Child Tax Credit

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Child tax credits

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award letter

Further information:

We can accept up to 100% of Child Tax Credit.

We require the awards letter as part of the application packaging. Where the child is 16 or over on the award letter, you shouldn't include this income unless you can evidence that the income will continue. ​

Where the income is being excluded and the child is no longer financially dependent, you can also exclude them as a dependent from the application.

Please note the customer(s) named on the award statement must match the application.

Click below for more information on:
• Working Tax Credits
• Universal Credit

Close

Income and packaging C - continued

Contractors Close
Close

Contractors

We support customers who are contractors and have dedicated information on our Contractors hub, including:

  • PAYE Contract Workers including Zero hour and Agency workers.
  • Construction Industry Scheme (CIS).
  • Umbrella Company Contractor.
  • High Income / Day Rate Contractors.
Close
Contractor- High Income Close
Close

Contractor- High Income

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Self-employed

Evidence:

Proof of Contract(s) for a 12 month period showing a minimum of 6 months completed.

Latest 3 months business and personal Bank Statements.

 

If paid through an Umbrella Company or Fixed short-term contract

Evidence:

Proof of Contract(s) for a 12 month period showing a minimum of 6 months completed.

Latest 3 months business and personal Bank Statements.

Latest Payslip (1 month OR 4 weeks).

 

Further information:

The above applies only to customers earning at least £75,000 per annum. Please refer to our Contractors Hub for full criteria and further application keying guidance.

Close
Commission Close

Commission

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Commission

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips showing commission payments. YTD income should support.

Where this is paid weekly the latest 13 payslips will be required.

Constant Attendance Allowance

Expenses Close
Close

Expenses

Acceptable?  No

Close
Foreign Currency Income Close
Close

Foreign Currency Income

We will use 100% of converted income and will not apply a haircut. For any foreign currency values other than GBP, the amount should be converted using an appropriate currency conversion tool and input as GBP in the application and will be subject to the exchange rate on the day of underwriter assessment. Please see below for our acceptable list of currencies:

 

 

Where all or part of an applicant's income used to make the mortgage repayments is paid to them in a foreign currency, the MCD requires advisers to provide additional point of sale risk disclosures. It also requires lenders to monitor the customer’s foreign exchange exposure and notify them when it deviates adversely by 20% or more from the exchange rate at the point that the mortgage was completed.

 

Please also see Working abroad.

Close
Foster Care Allowance Close
Close

Foster Care Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Other non-taxable income

Evidence: Local Authority confirmation of the last two years’ payments received, along with confirmation that the customer is still a registered Foster Carer from the Local Authority.

Further information:

HMRC generally treat foster carers as being self-employed. However, the SA302 is only used to determine any tax and NI payable after any qualifying amount has been deducted.

Investment Income (Regular)

Close

Job Seeker's Allowance (JSA)

Acceptable?  No

Income and packaging L-N

Locum work Close
Close

Locum work

Locum income paid on a PAYE basis, please see zero hours contract.

Locum income paid on a self-employed basis, please see self-employed.

Close
Long Term Large Town Allowance Close
Close

Long Term Large Town Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence for residential New Business and Additional Borrowing applications: 0-3 months payslips. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many we need.

 

Evidence for Buy To Let applications: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

 

Further information:

A variation of allowances can be received by customers to compensate for the location of their work e.g. Town Allowance, High Cost Area Allowance, Area Allowance etc.

Consideration should be given to whether the customer is relocating as part of the application, and whether the income will continue.

Close
Maintenance - Child or Court approved maintenance (spousal) Close
Close

Maintenance - Child or Court approved maintenance (spousal)

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Child Maintenance (for both child and spousal maintenance)

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Bank Statements. May need Court order (where applicable).

Further information:

Child maintenance can be accepted at 100%. This can either be court ordered or non-court ordered.

 

Court Approved Maintenance (spousal):

Can be accepted at 100%. This will be subject to underwriting and we may require sight of the court order.

• There is an expectation the payments will continue at the current level for at least the next 5 years and we may also need to understand how the customer will replace this income when it ends.

• We require up to 3 months bank statements to evidence this, please see your tailored packaging requirements upon submission of the Full Mortgage Application.

• The party paying the maintenance can’t be named on the bank account the maintenance is paid into.

• We can consider maintenance income paid by someone living abroad subject to the underwriters discretion, upon a Full Mortgage Application being submitted. This must be paid in an acceptable currency.

Close
Maternity Pay Close
Close

Maternity Pay

Please see 'Parental (Maternity/Paternity) Pay'.

Close
NHS Bank Work Close
Close

NHS Bank Work

NHS Bank Work with Main NHS Employment

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?''

Select option: Regular overtime

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. YTD income should support.

 

NHS Bank Work only

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips and latest P60

 

Further information:

This is received by medical staff for flexible shift work.

Where a customer receives ‘bank’ income in addition to their main NHS employment this should be treated as overtime (this may be evidenced on separate / additional payslips to the customer’s main income).

Where a customer is only in receipt of ‘bank’ income, this should be treated as Zero-Hour contractor income.

Close
Go up to A - Z

Income and packaging O-R

Overtime – Guaranteed Close
Close

Overtime – Guaranteed

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?''

Select option: Guaranteed overtime

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips showing overtime.

Where this is paid weekly the latest 13 payslips will be required.

Close
Overtime – Regular Close
Close

Overtime – Regular

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?''

Select option: Regular overtime

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips showing overtime.

Where this is paid weekly the latest 13 payslips will be required.

Close
Parental (Maternity/Paternity) Pay Close
Close

Parental (Maternity/Paternity) Pay

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence:

 

Returning to work on the same basis

We will use the customer's basic salary earned prior to going on maternity/paternity leave for affordability purposes.

In this scenario for residential applications we will need:

- If using basic earnings only: Latest 1 month payslip prior to maternity/paternity leave

- If using alongside a variable or other income type: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips prior to maternity/paternity leave

 

Returning to work on a different basis

We will use the customers future basic salary, based on a pro rata of the previous basic salary, prior to going on maternity/paternity leave, for affordability purposes.

In this scenario for residential applications we will need:

- If using basic earnings only, we will need a letter from employer confirming:

The customers new hours

Their new annual salary based on them returning on a different basis

When their new salary will take effect

Please note, this won’t be requested on the application portal as part of basic packaging, but we will always require this.

- If using alongside a variable or other income type: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips prior to maternity/paternity leave. Our underwriters reserve the right to also request a letter from the employer as detailed above.

For all Buy To Let applications we will need: Latest 3 months consecutive payslips prior to maternity/paternity leave and 3 months most recent bank statements. Our underwriters reserve the right to also request from you an employers letter, as detailed above, where returning to work on a different basis.

 

Further information:

When a customer is on maternity or paternity leave, it is important to understand the current and future circumstances for mortgage affordability purposes.

In all scenarios:

We require details of future childcare provision and costs, where applicable. You can use the customer declared amount, if known, or national averages.

Ensure to capture a known increase in under 18 financial dependants.

We reserve the right to request additional information if required.

Close
Pay rise/Change of Income or Job (Employed income only) Close
Close

Pay rise/Change of Income or Job (Employed income only)

Where the customer is expecting a pay rise or a pending incentive payment (e.g. bonus) or they are changing their job within the next 3 months, we can accept an employer’s letter or contract to confirm the new income in lieu of latest month’s payslip* 

The contract or letter will need to confirm:

• Customers name

• Date the income change takes effect from

• New Salary/Type of income e.g main salary or a bonus payment

• Where the change is an incentive payment, the terms of this incentive need to be clear as the customer must meet our minimum track record for the type of incentive payment e.g. for discretionary annual bonus the customer would need to be able to evidence a 2 year track record of this being received 

• If using an employer’s letter this must be on headed paper

*Where the customer has a student loan deduction, we can only use an increased basic income where there is one full payslip to evidence what the new student loan payment will be.

Note: Customers must meet our minimum employment length and we may require current payslips to evidence this. When you submit an application, the portal will confirm how many payslips we need. Our underwriters reserve the right to ask for additional evidence when considering any income change. 

Close
Pension (State) Close
Close

Pension (State)

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: State Pension

Evidence: Latest Bank Statement OR Pension Statement

Close
Pension (Private) Close
Close

Pension (Private)

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Private/employer Pension

Evidence for residential New Business and Additional Borrowing applications: 0-3 months payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

Only send in the specific documents requested on the portal.

 

Evidence for Buy To Let applications: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. See case packaging requirements on Full Mortgage Application as these could differ.

Close
Pension Credit Close
Close

Pension Credit

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Other taxable income

Evidence: Department of Work & Pensions Statement OR Latest Benefit Award Letter

Close
Personal Independence Payment (PIP) Close
Close

Personal Independence Payment (PIP)

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Personal Independance Payment

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award Letter

Close
Reduced Earnings Allowance Close
Close

Reduced Earnings Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Other non-taxable income

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award Letter or Latest 3 months Consecutive Bank Statements showing credits

Close
Rental Income Close
Close

Rental Income

Acceptable:  Yes (only on residential applications)

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Rental income

Evidence: 

• 2 years SA302’s / Tax assessments

• 2 years Tax Year Overviews.

Further information:

We will use the net amount from ‘Profit from UK Land & Property’ minus ‘finance costs’ (evidenced on the tax document), using the latest year or average of last 2 years, whichever is lower. For full guidance on how to do this, please see our rental income guidance.

Note - Where rental income is not being used for affordability, please see Background Buy to Lets

Where the customer has purchased a new rental property since the latest tax return:

• If the property is self-financing (rental income covers the mortgage payment) we will still use the rental income as per the tax returns.

• Should the property run at a loss e.g. the rental income received is less than the mortgage payment creating a shortfall, please key the shortfall amount on the application under Expenditure > Other financial commitments.

If the customer has sold a rental property or part of their portfolio since the latest tax return, we will be unable to use any rental income.

Close

Income and packaging S

Scottish Adult/Child Disability Payment Close
Close

Scottish Adult/Child Disability Payment

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Disability Living Allowance

Close

Scottish Child Payment

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Child benefit

Evidence: Latest Benefit Award Letter or Latest 3 months Consecutive Bank Statements showing credits

Further information: This income can be received alongside Child Benefit income.

Where the child is 16 or over on the award letter, you shouldn't include this income unless you can evidence that the income will continue. ​

Where the income is being excluded and the child is no longer financially dependent, you can also exclude them as a dependent from the application.

Close
Scottish Recommended Allowance Close
Close

Scottish Recommended Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Other non-taxable income

Evidence: Local Authority confirmation of the last two years’ payments received, along with confirmation that the customer is still a registered Foster Carer from the Local Authority.

Further information:

We can consider income that is received by Foster Carers.

HMRC generally treat foster carers as being self-employed. However, the SA302 is only used to determine any tax and NI payable after any qualifying amount has been deducted.

Close
Second job Close
Close

Second job

Acceptable: Yes*​

On the application, key in: Main employment section​

Select option: Key according to relevant income type received e.g.: Employed or Employed contractor​

Evidence:​

Basic salary: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips​

See relevant income type e.g.: Contract PAYE.​

 

Self-employed:​

 

Sole Traders or Partnerships​

Acceptable?  Yes​

Main or Other: Main​

Key on application as: Self-employed​

Evidence: ​

  • For Sole Traders evidence, please see self-employed.

  • For Partnerships evidence, please see self-employed.

​
See Sole Traders or Partnerships on our Self-employed Hub for more information.​

​

Limited partnership or Limited Liability partnership​

Acceptable?  Yes​

Main or Other: Main​

Key on application as: Self-employed​

Evidence: ​

  • For Limited partnership or Limited Liability partnership evidence, please see self-employed.

​
See Partnerships on our Self-employed Hub for more information.​

​

Limited Company​

Acceptable?  Yes​

Main or Other: Main​

Key on application as: Self-employed​

Evidence:​

  • For Limited Company evidence, please see self-employed.

​

See Limited Company on our Self-employed Hub for more information.​

​

​

Further information or income evidence may be requested by the underwriter during the application process.​

​

Further information:​

We can use up to 100% of second job income.​

​

*We require a minimum of 6 months employment to utilise the income from a 2nd job. We will require a longer track record where the second job is not a permanent PAYE contract.​

​

We can consider income from more than 2 jobs and would need to understand the overall sustainability of the income ie: amount of hours worked each week.​

Close
Seafarers Close
Close

Seafarers

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed or Self-employed according to relevant income type received

Evidence:

Tax paid at source: Payslip(s)*

Tax not paid at source: Payslip(s)* and Latest SA302.

*Number of payslips will depend on income type - refer to applicable income type e.g. Contractor (PAYE).

Further information:

We can consider Seafarers income if this is paid in an acceptable currency to NatWest and can be evidenced via payslips and where tax is not paid at source, the most recent SA302 to confirm that the client is registered for UK tax.

The client must also have permanent right to reside in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status or Irish Citizenship AND must live in the UK when not at sea.

The evidence requirements will depend on the customer's employment type.

Close
Self-employed Close
Close

Self-employed

  • Tax Returns that are shown as ‘in progress’ won’t be accepted. We can accept accounts / tax returns submitted on their accountants’ software.​

  • The year end on the accounts / tax returns must be the most recent and must not be dated more than 18 months before the date of application.​

​

Sole Traders​

Acceptable: Yes​

On the application, key in: Main employment section​

Select option: Self-employed and select Sole trader when asked Business type​

Evidence:​

  • 2 years SA302 or 100% Tax Calculations, and Tax year overview(s).​

​
See Sole Traders on our Self-employed Hub for more information.​

​

Partnerships​

Simple or Limited Liability Partnership​

Acceptable: Yes​

On the application, key in: Main employment section​

Select option: Self-employed and select Partnership when asked Business type​

Evidence:​

  • 2 years SA302 or 100% Tax Calculations, and Tax year overview(s).​

​

See Partnerships on our Self-employed Hub for more information.​

​

Limited Company​

Acceptable: Yes​

On the application, key in: Main employment section​

Select option: Self-employed and select Limited company when asked Business type​

Evidence:​

  • 1 year Full and Finalised accounts showing 2 years’ trading figures (Draft accounts are not acceptable).​

  • 2 years SA302 or 100% Tax Calculations, and Tax year overview(s).​

 

See Limited Company on our Self-employed Hub for more information.

Close
Shift Allowance - Regular Close
Close

Shift Allowance - Regular

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Shift allowance

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips. YTD income should support

Further Information: A variation of allowances can be received by customers to compensate for additional responsibilities / working hours.

 

Common examples may include but not limited to:

•             Additional Duty Hours. 

•             Call Out.

•             Danger Allowance.

•             Offshore Allowance.

•             On Call. 

•             Sleepover Allowance.

•             Standby.

•             Unsocial Hours.

•             WTD (Working Time Directive). 

Working from Home Allowance is not Acceptable.

Close
Stipend Close
Close

Stipend

Acceptable? Yes

On the application, key in: Other income section

Select option: Other taxable income

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips

Further information:

We can consider stipend income where the long term nature of the income can be evidenced.

Stipends are commonly paid to clients who are studying their PhD, are in a religious role or working for a university or a scholarship. This is not an exhaustive list and others can be considered.

Close
Go up to A - Z

Income and packaging T

Third Party Income Close
Close

Third Party Income

Acceptable? No

Close
Tips/Tronc Close
Close

Tips/Tronc

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Main employment section when asked 'Does the applicant receive an additional income from this employment?'

Select option: Commission

Evidence: Latest 3 months consecutive Payslips showing the payments. YTD income should support.

Where this is paid weekly the latest 13 payslips will be required.

Close
Temporary Agency Income Close
Close

Temporary Agency Income

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed contractor

Evidence: Latest 12 months consecutive Payslips & employers reference

Close
Trust Income (Regular) Close
Close

Trust Income (Regular)

Acceptable: Yes

On the application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Trust

Evidence: Latest 6 months consecutive evidence (e.g. bank statements or payslips) & letter of confirmation

Close
Go up to A - Z

Income and packaging U-Z

Universal Credit Close
Close

Universal Credit

Acceptable?  Yes

On the application, key in:  Other income section

Select option: Universal Credit

Evidence: Latest benefit statement or award letter

Further information:

Only the following elements of Universal Credit can be included as income:

  • Standard Allowance: Can only be considered when it can be evidenced from the award letter that the customer is employed - for example the award letter details a take home pay. This will confirm that the customer is in receipt of Working Tax Credits and employment support allowance and not unemployment benefits. If the take-home pay shows as £0 on the statement, the standard allowance can’t be used for mortgage purposes.

  • Carers Element

  • Child Element

  • Disabled Child Element

  • Childcare Costs Element

Housing Element - This should be deducted from the monthly payment figure and not included for mortgage affordability purposes.

The customer must provide their Universal Credit statement(s). This must include a full breakdown of this income. Circumstances are assessed each month meaning the amount paid may change.

Please note the customer(s) named on the award statement must match the application.

Close
War Widow(er) Pension Close
Close

War Widow(er) Pension

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other income section

Select option: State Pension

Evidence: Latest Bank Statement or Pension Statement

Close
Widowed Parents Allowance Close
Close

Widowed Parents Allowance

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Other taxable income

Evidence: Latest Bank Statement or Pension Statement

Close
Working Tax Credit Close
Close

Working Tax Credit

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Other Income section

Select option: Working Families Tax Credit

Evidence: Latest Benefit award letter

Further information:

We can accept up to 100% of working tax credits.

Please note the customer(s) named on the award statement must match the application.

Close
Zero Hours Contract Close
Close

Zero Hours Contract

Acceptable: Yes

On application, key in: Main employment section

Select option: Employed

Evidence: Income can be verified using the latest 3 months' payslips (total averaged and in line with the YTD figure) and latest P60. They must demonstrate consistent levels of income.

Further information:

There must be a history of employment over the past 12 months and confirmation that a current contract is in place.

Close
Go up to A - Z

Proof of deposit

Below is what we'll require for proof of deposit for purchase applications.

  • Builder’s incentives
  • Gifted deposit letter
  • Inherited Property
  • Forces Help to Buy Scheme

  • Foreign Nationals

Name and address identification

We will electronically verify name and address of your client in the majority of cases however if we do require proof you will be informed at the end of the mortgage submission. In these cases please provide one of the following:

Name identification

  • Passport.
  • Full UK driving licence.
  • UK provisional driving licence.
  • Military ID card.
  • EU/EEA National ID card.
  • EU/EEA driving licence.

Address identification

  • Bank statement (not NatWest).
  • Council tax bill.
  • Utility bill / statement.
  • Mortgage statement/solicitor mortgage correspondence.
  • Local council/housing authority tenancy agreement.
  • Credit union statement.
  • Full UK driving licence.
  • UK provisional driving licence.
  • HMRC letter (Notice of Tax Coding or National Insurance Confirmation Letter)
